# Faultsense — Full Reference

> Faultsense is a lightweight (6.5KB gzipped), zero-dependency browser agent that validates feature correctness in production through declarative HTML attribute assertions. It runs against real user sessions — like E2E test assertions built into your HTML.

- Docs: https://www.faultsense.org/docs
- Examples: https://www.faultsense.org/examples
- GitHub: https://github.com/Fault-Sense/faultsense-agent

## Core Concept

Developers add `fs-*` attributes to HTML elements to declare: (1) what user event triggers the assertion, (2) what DOM outcome is expected, and (3) optional inline modifiers to refine the check. The agent monitors real user sessions and reports pass/fail to a collector.

An element with multiple `fs-assert-*` attributes creates one assertion per type. Each is resolved independently.

---

## API Reference

### Required Attributes

| Attribute | Description |
|---|---|
| `fs-assert="<key>"` | Assertion key. Use `/` to group hierarchically (e.g., `"checkout/submit-order"`) |
| `fs-trigger="<value>"` | Event that triggers the assertion (exactly one per element) |

### Assertion Key Convention

Use `/` separators to group related assertions, like file paths or package names. Nest as deep as needed:

```
fs-assert="checkout/add-to-cart"
fs-assert="checkout/submit-order"
fs-assert="profile/media/upload-photo"
fs-assert="auth/login"
```

Keys must be stable across releases. Human-readable labels are configured on the collector side, not in the HTML.

### Triggers

`fs-trigger="<value>"` — Exactly one required per element.

| Trigger | When it fires | Typical elements |
|---|---|---|
| `click` | Element is clicked | button, a, div, span |
| `dblclick` | Element is double-clicked | Any |
| `change` | Input value changes | input, select, textarea, checkbox |
| `blur` | Element loses focus | input, textarea |
| `submit` | Form is submitted | form |
| `mount` | Element is added to the DOM | Any (useful for page load validation) |
| `unmount` | Element is removed from the DOM | Any |
| `load` | Resource finishes loading | img, video, iframe |
| `error` | Resource fails to load | img, video, iframe |
| `invariant` | Continuously monitors — only reports failures and recoveries | Any (best with `visible`/`hidden`) |
| `hover` | Mouse enters the element (alias for `mouseenter`) | Any |
| `focus` | Element receives focus (alias for `focusin`) | input, button, a |
| `input` | Value changes while typing | input, textarea |
| `keydown` | Any key is pressed | Any focusable element |
| `keydown:<key>` | Specific key pressed (e.g., `keydown:Escape`, `keydown:ctrl+s`) | Any focusable element |
| `online` | Browser connectivity restored | Any |
| `offline` | Browser connectivity lost | Any |

### Assertion Types

At least one required. Value is a CSS selector, optionally with inline modifiers in brackets.

**DOM Assertions:**

| Attribute | Resolves when |
|---|---|
| `fs-assert-added="<selector>"` | A new element matching the selector appears in the DOM |
| `fs-assert-removed="<selector>"` | An element matching the selector is removed from the DOM |
| `fs-assert-updated="<selector>"` | The matched element or its subtree is mutated (text, attributes, children) |
| `fs-assert-visible="<selector>"` | The matched element exists and has layout dimensions |
| `fs-assert-hidden="<selector>"` | The matched element exists but has no layout dimensions |
| `fs-assert-loaded="<selector>"` | A media element (img/video) matching the selector finishes loading |
| `fs-assert-stable="<selector>"` | The matched element's subtree is NOT mutated during the timeout window (inverted `updated`) |

**Sequence Assertions:**

| Attribute | Resolves when |
|---|---|
| `fs-assert-after="<key>"` | All referenced parent assertion keys have already passed. Comma-separated for multiple (AND semantics). |

`after` resolves immediately at creation time — it either passes (all parents passed) or fails (at least one hasn't). Produces an independent data point alongside any DOM/route assertions on the same element. Failed `after` assertions recover on re-trigger if parents have since passed.

**Conditional Assertions** — append a condition key to the type name:

`fs-assert-{type}-{condition-key}="<selector>"`

| Component | Description | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| `{type}` | DOM assertion type | `added`, `removed`, `updated`, `visible`, `hidden` |
| `{condition-key}` | Freeform label for this outcome | `success`, `error`, `empty`, `rate-limited` |

Multiple condition keys on the same element and type form a sibling group. The first conditional whose selector matches resolves the group — others are silently dismissed. If no condition matches before timeout, one group failure is reported.

Example:
```html
<button fs-assert-added-success=".dashboard"
        fs-assert-added-error=".error-msg" ...>
```

### Inline Modifiers

Modifiers are chained in the attribute value using CSS-like bracket syntax after the selector:

```html
fs-assert-updated='#count[text-matches=\d+]'
fs-assert-updated='#logo[src=/img/new.png][alt=New Logo]'
fs-assert-updated='.panel[classlist=active:true,hidden:false]'
fs-assert-added-success='.success[text-matches=Order #\d+]'
```

**Reserved modifier keys:**

| Key | Description |
|---|---|
| `[text-matches=<pattern>]` | Text content must match (regex, **partial match** — unanchored). Evaluated as `new RegExp(value).test(el.textContent)`. Use `^exact$` to anchor. |
| `[value-matches=<pattern>]` | Form control `.value` property must match (regex, **partial match** — unanchored). Works on `input`, `textarea`, `select`. Reads the live DOM property, not the HTML attribute. Use `^exact$` to anchor. |
| `[checked=true\|false]` | Checkbox/radio `.checked` DOM property. Reads the live boolean state, not the HTML `checked` attribute. |
| `[disabled=true\|false]` | Disabled state. Checks native `.disabled` property (form elements) and `aria-disabled="true"` attribute. |
| `[focused=true\|false]` | Focus state. Checks `document.activeElement === el`. Same MutationObserver caveat as `value-matches` — evaluates during event-triggered resolution. |
| `[focused-within=true\|false]` | Focus-within state. Checks `el.matches(':focus-within')` — true when the element or any descendant has focus. |
| `[count=N]` | Exactly N elements must match the selector (`querySelectorAll(selector).length === N`). |
| `[count-min=N]` | At least N elements must match the selector. |
| `[count-max=N]` | At most N elements must match the selector. |
| `[classlist=<class:bool,...>]` | Classes on the element. Format: `active:true,hidden:false` where `true` = must have, `false` = must not have. |

**Unreserved keys** are treated as attribute checks (regex, **full match** — auto-anchored with `^(?:...)$`):

| Example | Checks |
|---|---|
| `[src=/img/logo.png]` | Element has `src` exactly matching `/img/logo.png` |
| `[data-state=active]` | Element has `data-state` exactly matching `"active"` |
| `[data-state=active\|ready]` | Element has `data-state` matching `"active"` or `"ready"` |
| `[data-status=success-\d+]` | Element has `data-status` matching `"success-"` followed by digits |
| `[aria-expanded=true]` | Element has `aria-expanded="true"` |

Multiple attribute checks can be chained: `#logo[src=/img/new.png][width=100][alt=Logo]`

**Regex anchoring summary:**
- `text-matches` and `value-matches`: **partial match** (unanchored) — pattern can match anywhere in the string
- Attribute checks (unreserved keys) and `route`: **full match** (auto-anchored) — pattern must match the entire value

### Element-Level Attributes

These apply to the element, not to a specific assertion type:

| Attribute | Description |
|---|---|
| `fs-assert-timeout="<ms>"` | Override the default timeout (1000ms). Assertion fails if not resolved within this time. |
| `fs-assert-mpa="true"` | Multi-page assertion. Persisted to localStorage and resolved on the next page load. |
| `fs-assert-grouped=""` | Group all conditional assertions on this element as siblings regardless of type. Use for mutually exclusive outcomes that need different assertion types (e.g., `removed-success` + `added-error`). |

### Conditional Assertions

Conditional assertions use a `{condition-key}` suffix to define multiple possible outcomes for the same assertion type:

```html
<button fs-assert="auth/login" fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-added-success=".dashboard"
  fs-assert-added-error=".error-msg">Login</button>
```

Multiple conditionals on the same element and type form a **sibling group**. The first conditional whose selector matches resolves the group — others are dismissed. Condition keys are freeform lowercase alphanumeric strings with hyphens (e.g., `success`, `error`, `empty`, `rate-limited`). No server-side integration required.

**Cross-type grouping (`fs-assert-grouped=""`):** By default, siblings are scoped to `assertionKey + type`. Add `fs-assert-grouped=""` when mutually exclusive outcomes need different assertion types:

```html
<button fs-assert="todos/remove-item" fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-grouped=""
  fs-assert-removed-success=".todo-item"
  fs-assert-added-error=".error-msg"
  fs-assert-timeout="5000">Delete</button>
```

### Out-of-Band (OOB) Assertions

OOB assertions fire when a referenced parent assertion passes or fails. Use for side-effect elements (count labels, totals, toasts, error indicators) that should be verified after a primary action resolves, without prop drilling.

`fs-assert-oob` and `fs-assert-oob-fail` replace `fs-trigger` on OOB elements. Assertion types are declared normally via `fs-assert-{type}`.

```html
<!-- Primary action -->
<button fs-assert="todos/add-item" fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-added-success=".todo-item"
  fs-assert-added-error=".add-error">Add</button>

<!-- OOB: count should reflect the new total after add succeeds -->
<div id="todo-count"
  fs-assert="todos/count-updated"
  fs-assert-oob="todos/add-item"
  fs-assert-visible="[text-matches=\d+/\d+ remaining]">
  2/3 remaining
</div>

<!-- OOB-fail: verify error indicator shown when add fails -->
<div id="error-check"
  fs-assert="todos/add-error-check"
  fs-assert-oob-fail="todos/add-item"
  fs-assert-visible=".error-indicator">
</div>
```

- `fs-assert-oob="key1,key2"` — fires when any listed parent assertion **passes**
- `fs-assert-oob-fail="key1,key2"` — fires when any listed parent assertion **fails** (timeout, GC, SLA). Dismissed assertions (losing conditional siblings) do NOT trigger oob-fail.
- Both can coexist on the same element as independent triggers
- Multiple parent keys are comma-separated (OR — fires if any parent matches)
- No chaining: OOB passing does not trigger further OOB
- Selector is optional — omit for self-referencing (checks the OOB element itself)
- **Use state assertions** (`visible`, `hidden`, `added`, `removed`) with OOB. Event types (`updated`, `loaded`) require witnessing a mutation and will miss changes that already occurred before the OOB assertion is created.

**Multi-check conditional outcomes:** To verify multiple things on one conditional success (e.g., delete removes the row AND shows a toast), use OOB on the secondary element:

```html
<!-- Primary: conditional on the trigger -->
<button fs-assert="todos/remove-item" fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-grouped=""
  fs-assert-removed-success=".todo-item"
  fs-assert-added-error=".error-msg">Delete</button>

<!-- Secondary: OOB checks toast appeared after successful delete -->
<div class="toast-container"
  fs-assert="todos/delete-toast"
  fs-assert-oob="todos/remove-item"
  fs-assert-visible=".success-toast">
</div>
```

The conditional handles the primary outcome check; OOB handles secondary checks triggered by the conditional's success. This avoids needing AND semantics within condition keys.

### Invariant Assertions

Invariants monitor conditions that should always hold — catching failures without user action (CSS regressions, race conditions, deploy breakage).

```html
<nav id="main-nav"
  fs-assert="layout/nav-visible"
  fs-trigger="invariant"
  fs-assert-visible="#main-nav">
</nav>
```

- Invariants stay **pending** — no collector traffic while the condition holds
- Only **failures** (violations) and **recoveries** (pass after failure) are reported
- On page unload, pending (never-violated) invariants are auto-passed as the "all clear" signal
- No timeout — invariants are perpetual for the page lifetime
- Best with state types (`visible`, `hidden`). Event types (`updated`, `loaded`) are allowed but warned against in debug mode.
- Invariants do not support conditional keys or MPA mode

### Stable Assertions

Stable assertions pass when the target element's subtree is NOT mutated during the timeout window. The temporal inverse of `updated`: any mutation fails immediately, timeout expiry with no mutations passes.

```html
<!-- OOB: verify price display doesn't flicker after add-to-cart -->
<div
  fs-assert="cart/price-stable"
  fs-assert-oob="cart/add-item"
  fs-assert-stable="#price-total"
  fs-assert-timeout="500">
</div>

<!-- Perpetual: this element should never be mutated -->
<div id="legal-notice"
  fs-assert="layout/legal-stable"
  fs-trigger="invariant"
  fs-assert-stable="#legal-notice">
</div>
```

- **Best with OOB**: Start the stability window after an expected mutation passes. Direct triggers work but will fail on the expected mutation itself.
- **Works with `invariant`**: Perpetual "never mutate" monitoring. Fails on any mutation, re-arms after failure.
- **Without `fs-assert-timeout`**: Passes via GC sweep (default 30s). Use explicit timeout for tighter stability windows.
- **Production bugs caught**: Flickering UI, React double-render loops, optimistic UI rollbacks, WebSocket duplicate renders.

---

## Placement Rules

1. **Attributes go on the trigger element** — the element the user directly interacts with. Only the exact `event.target` is processed.
2. **For forms**: Use `fs-trigger="submit"` on the `<form>` element, OR `fs-trigger="click"` on the submit `<button>`.
3. **For mount/unmount**: Place on the element being observed for addition/removal.
4. **For load/error**: Place on the media element itself (`<img>`, `<video>`, `<iframe>`).
5. **One trigger per element**: `fs-trigger` accepts exactly one value.
6. **Multiple assertion types on one element**: Valid. Each creates a separate assertion resolved independently.
7. **Framework components**: `fs-*` attributes must reach the actual DOM element. In React, native elements pass through unknown attributes. Wrapper components need to forward props.

---

## Common Patterns

### 1. Button Click → DOM Update (Counter)

```html
<button
  fs-assert="counter/increment"
  fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-updated='#counter[text-matches=Count: [1-9]\d*]'>
  Increment
</button>
<div id="counter">Count: 0</div>
```

Asserts: clicking increments the count and the display updates with a positive number.

### 2. Form Submit → Element Added (Todo)

```html
<button type="submit"
  fs-assert="todo/add-item"
  fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-added=".todo-item">
  Add Todo
</button>
```

Asserts: submitting adds a new todo item to the DOM.

### 3. Modal Open/Close (Dialog)

```html
<!-- Open trigger -->
<button
  fs-assert="modal/open"
  fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-visible=".modal-overlay">
  Open Modal
</button>

<!-- Close trigger (inside modal) -->
<button
  fs-assert="modal/close"
  fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-removed=".modal-content">
  Cancel
</button>
```

Asserts: clicking opens the modal (visible), clicking cancel removes it.

### 4. Tab Switching (Tabbed Interface)

```html
<button class="tab-button" data-tab="settings"
  fs-assert="tabs/switch-tab"
  fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-visible=".tab-content[data-tab='settings']">
  Settings
</button>
```

Asserts: clicking the tab makes the corresponding content panel visible.

### 5. Multi-Step Wizard with Sequence Validation

```html
<!-- Step 1 -->
<button fs-assert="wizard/step-1" fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-visible=".wizard-step[data-step='2']">
  Next
</button>

<!-- Step 2: must have completed step 1 -->
<button fs-assert="wizard/step-2" fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-after="wizard/step-1"
  fs-assert-visible=".wizard-step[data-step='3']">
  Next
</button>

<!-- Step 3: must have completed step 2 -->
<button fs-assert="wizard/step-3" fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-after="wizard/step-2"
  fs-assert-visible=".confirmation">
  Submit
</button>
```

Asserts: each step shows the correct content AND validates the user completed the previous step. `after` and `visible` are independent assertions — a sequence violation with correct UI is a visible finding.

### 6. Form Submit → Conditional Assertions

```html
<form
  fs-assert="contact/submit-form"
  fs-trigger="submit"
  fs-assert-added-success=".success-msg"
  fs-assert-added-validation-error=".validation-errors"
  fs-assert-added-server-error=".server-error"
  fs-assert-timeout="2000">
  <input name="email" type="email" />
  <button type="submit">Send</button>
</form>
```

Asserts: if success message appears, the success conditional passes. If validation errors appear, that conditional passes. If server error appears, that conditional passes. Other siblings are dismissed. No server-side integration needed.

### 7. MPA Navigation (Multi-Page App)

```html
<button
  fs-assert="mpa-form/submit"
  fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-mpa="true"
  fs-assert-visible=".success-message">
  Submit
</button>
```

Asserts: after a full page reload, the success message is visible. The assertion is stored in localStorage and resolved when the agent initializes on the next page.

### 8. Multiple Assertion Types on One Trigger

```html
<!-- Toggle asserts both classlist change AND edit button disabled state -->
<input type="checkbox"
  fs-assert="todos/toggle-complete"
  fs-trigger="change"
  fs-assert-updated='.todo-item[classlist=completed:true]'
  fs-assert-visible='#edit-btn-1[disabled=true]' />
```

Each `fs-assert-{type}` attribute creates a separate assertion from the same trigger. Both resolve independently. Use specific selectors (ids) to target the right element in lists.

### 9. Count Assertions (Cardinality)

```html
<!-- After add, verify at least 1 result exists -->
<button fs-assert="search/execute" fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-added=".result-card[count-min=1]">Search</button>

<!-- OOB: verify total todo count matches expected after add/remove -->
<div
  fs-assert="todos/item-count"
  fs-assert-oob="todos/add-item,todos/remove-item"
  fs-assert-visible=".todo-item[count=5]">
</div>
```

`count` checks `querySelectorAll(selector).length` before per-element modifiers run. Combine with other modifiers: `.item[count=3][text-matches=\w+]` means "3 elements match `.item`, and at least one has matching text."

### 10. Data Load → Conditional DOM Update

```html
<button
  fs-assert="data/load-posts"
  fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-updated-success="#results"
  fs-assert-added-error=".error"
  fs-assert-timeout="2600">
  Load Posts
</button>
<div id="results"></div>
```

Asserts: if the results container updates, the success conditional passes. If an error element appears, that conditional passes. No server-side integration needed.

---

## Framework Syntax

The `fs-*` attributes are standard HTML attributes. They work in any framework that renders to the DOM.

### Plain HTML

```html
<button fs-assert="cart/add-item" fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-updated="#cart-count">
  Add to Cart
</button>
```

### React JSX

```jsx
<button fs-assert="cart/add-item" fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-updated="#cart-count"
  onClick={handleAddToCart}>
  Add to Cart
</button>
```

React passes unknown attributes through to the DOM on native elements. For custom components, ensure props are forwarded to the root DOM element.

**Important:** React drops custom attributes with boolean `true` values. Use `fs-assert-grouped=""` (with explicit empty string) not bare `fs-assert-grouped` in JSX.

### Vue SFC

```vue
<template>
  <button fs-assert="cart/add-item" fs-trigger="click"
    fs-assert-updated="#cart-count"
    @click="handleAddToCart">
    Add to Cart
  </button>
</template>
```

Vue passes unknown attributes through via `inheritAttrs` (default: true).

### Svelte

```svelte
<button fs-assert="cart/add-item" fs-trigger="click"
  fs-assert-updated="#cart-count"
  on:click={handleAddToCart}>
  Add to Cart
</button>
```

Svelte passes unknown attributes through on native elements.

---

## Common Mistakes

1. **Putting `fs-trigger` on a parent instead of the interacted element.** The agent only processes the exact `event.target`. If the user clicks a `<span>` inside a `<button>`, the `<button>` is the target (due to event bubbling), but a `<div>` wrapper above it is not.

2. **Using reserved words as condition keys.** Avoid using assertion type names (`added`, `removed`, `updated`, `visible`, `hidden`, `loaded`, `oob`, `oob-fail`) as condition keys — they create confusing attribute names like `fs-assert-added-removed`.

3. **Using `added` when `updated` is correct.** Use `added` when the element doesn't exist yet and will be created. Use `updated` when the element already exists and its content/attributes will change.

4. **Using `visible` when `added` is correct.** `visible` checks an existing element's layout dimensions. `added` checks for a new element appearing in the DOM. If the element is conditionally rendered (doesn't exist until triggered), use `added`.

5. **Missing required attributes.** Every instrumented element needs: `fs-assert`, `fs-trigger`, and at least one assertion type.

6. **Using `updated` or `loaded` with OOB or invariant assertions.** OOB and invariant assertions check current DOM state. Event-based types (`updated`, `loaded`) require witnessing a mutation/event and will miss changes that already occurred. Use state-based types (`visible`, `hidden`, `added`, `removed`).

7. **Using bare boolean attributes in React JSX.** React drops custom attributes with boolean `true` values. Write `fs-assert-grouped=""` not `fs-assert-grouped` in JSX. This applies to any `fs-*` attribute used as a boolean flag.

8. **Using `text-matches` with exact dynamic values.** If the expected text is dynamic (user names, prices, timestamps), use a regex pattern instead of an exact string. `[text-matches=\\d+]` is better than `[text-matches=42]`.

9. **Putting multiple assertion types under the same condition key for AND logic.** `fs-assert-removed-success` and `fs-assert-visible-success` on the same element are siblings (first to resolve dismisses the other), NOT AND. For multiple checks on one outcome, use OOB: the primary check goes on the trigger element, secondary checks go on OOB elements that fire when the primary passes.

10. **Using CSS attribute selectors in inline modifier values.** The bracket parser treats `[` as a modifier delimiter. A selector like `[data-id="123"] .btn[disabled=true]` will be misparsed — `[data-id="123"]` is consumed as a modifier, not as part of the CSS selector. Use id selectors (`#btn-123[disabled=true]`) or class selectors instead.

11. **Using OOB for per-item assertions in lists.** OOB broadcasts to ALL elements matching `fs-assert-oob="key"`. In a list of items, every item's OOB fires when any item's parent assertion passes — not just the acted-on item. Use OOB for singleton elements (count displays, toasts). For per-item state, use multiple assertion types on the trigger element instead.

12. **Using `count` with self-referencing (no selector).** A self-referencing assertion checks the element itself — count of self is always 1. `count` requires an explicit selector to be meaningful.

13. **Using `value-matches` and expecting MutationObserver to detect `.value` changes.** The `.value` DOM property is not an HTML attribute — typing into an input does NOT trigger MutationObserver. `value-matches` resolves reliably with event triggers (`change`, `blur`) or when the element also has a DOM mutation (attribute change, class change).

14. **Confusing `checked` with `value-matches` on checkboxes.** `el.value` on a checkbox returns the static `value` attribute (e.g., `"on"`), not the checked state. Use `[checked=true]` for checkbox/radio state, `[value-matches=...]` for input text values.

15. **Using `fs-assert-after` with `fs-trigger="invariant"`.** Invariants skip immediate resolution, so `after` would never be checked. Use `after` with event triggers (`click`, `submit`, etc.) or `mount`.

---

## Event Payload

Each resolved assertion sends this payload to the collector:

```typescript
{
  assertion_key: string;        // from fs-assert (e.g., "checkout/submit-order")
  assertion_trigger: string;    // from fs-trigger
  assertion_type: string;       // "added" | "removed" | "updated" | "visible" | "hidden" | "loaded" | "stable" | "route" | "after"
  assertion_type_value: string; // the CSS selector
  assertion_type_modifiers: {}; // modifier key-value pairs
  condition_key: string;        // condition key (e.g., "success", "error") or "" if unconditional
  element_snapshot: string;     // outerHTML of the trigger element at assertion creation time
  release_label: string;        // from configuration
  status: "passed" | "failed";
  status_reason: string;        // failure explanation (or "")
  timestamp: string;            // ISO 8601 timestamp
}
```

---

## Installation

### Script tag (auto-init)

```html
<script defer id="fs-agent"
  src="https://unpkg.com/faultsense@latest/dist/faultsense-agent.min.js"
  data-release-label="0.0.0"
  data-collector-url="console"
  data-debug="true">
</script>
```

### Manual init

```html
<script src="https://unpkg.com/faultsense@latest/dist/faultsense-agent.min.js"></script>
<script>
  Faultsense.init({
    releaseLabel: '0.0.0',
    collectorURL: Faultsense.collectors.consoleCollector,
    debug: true
  });
</script>
```

### Configuration

| Option | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `releaseLabel` | string | Yes | — | App version or commit hash |
| `collectorURL` | string or function | Yes | — | Backend endpoint URL or custom collector function |
| `apiKey` | string | If URL | — | API key (required when collectorURL is a URL) |
| `timeout` | number | No | 1000 | Default assertion timeout in ms |
| `debug` | boolean | No | false | Enable console logging |
